property.name: 17477a69-d9a5-4251-9329-0963e8121b5f
property.value:
### Designer Scripting — Stereotype DEFINITIONS
This is an ADDENDUM to the `get_designer_script_api` tool response (the `run_designer_script` cheat-sheet), covering how to author stereotype definitions (the metamodel). Same rules as the base cheat-sheet: plain JavaScript with top-level `await`, hold references in locals, never invent GUIDs, statement order IS operation order, the whole script is one undo composite, and you read the scoped `errors[]` after every run. Use exactly the members listed here — don't guess; the complete declarations are in `core.context.types.d.ts` (`IStereotypeDefinitionApi` / `IStereotypePropertyDefinitionApi`) if you ever need more.
#### Applying vs defining — don't confuse the two
The base cheat-sheet's "Stereotypes & associations" section APPLIES an existing stereotype to an element (`el.ensureStereotype("Audit").setProperty(...)`). THIS addendum DEFINES the stereotype itself — its name, what it can be applied to, and which properties it carries.
#### 1. Where definitions live, and how to read / create them
A stereotype definition is a child of a PACKAGE or an ELEMENT. Both owners expose the SAME four members — read with `getStereotypeDefinition(s)` / `hasStereotypeDefinition`, create with `addStereotypeDefinition`:
```js pkg.getStereotypeDefinitions() // ARRAY of definitions directly under this package/element pkg.getStereotypeDefinition("Audit") // a SINGLE one by name or id (null when none) pkg.hasStereotypeDefinition("Audit") pkg.addStereotypeDefinition("Audit") // create + return the new IStereotypeDefinitionApi ```
(Get a package handle with `lookupPackage(name)` / `getPackages()`; an element handle the usual way.)
DISCOVER existing definitions with the `get_designer_stereotype_definitions` tool before authoring, and get the element-settings type NAMES used for targeting from `get_designer_schema`'s "Element types" block.
IDEMPOTENT create idiom — there is no `ensureStereotypeDefinition`, so do it by hand (safe to re-run):
```js const def = pkg.getStereotypeDefinition("Audit") ?? pkg.addStereotypeDefinition("Audit"); ```
#### 2. The definition (`IStereotypeDefinitionApi`)
Every readonly field has a matching `setX()`.
```js def.setName(v) def.setComment(v) def.setHint(v) // identity / docs def.setIcon("fa-shield") // "fa-*", a URL, or a JSON icon model; null clears def.setIsTrait(b) def.setAllowMultipleApplies(b) def.setOrder(i) ```
**Targeting** — what the stereotype may be applied to. `setTargetMode` picks the strategy; some modes need a companion setter (the values are element-settings type NAMES or ids, resolved from `get_designer_schema`):
```js def.setTargetMode("all-elements") // anything def.setTargetMode("elements-of-type"); def.setTargetTypes(["Class", "Attribute"]); // these types def.setTargetMode("elements-that-reference"); def.setTargetReferenceTypes(["Class"]); // elements referencing these def.setTargetMode("filter-function"); def.setTargetFilterFunction("element => element.getName().endsWith('Dto')"); ```
**Apply mode** — when it gets applied: `"manually"` (user adds it) | `"on-element-created"` | `"always"`:
```js def.setApplyMode("manually") ```
**Display** on the diagram/tree (all optional):
```js def.setDisplayAsText(b) def.setDisplayAsAnnotation(b) def.setDisplayTextFunction(js) def.setDisplayIconFunction(js) ```
**Lifecycle hooks** — JS run on the applied stereotype (advanced): `setOnAppliedScript` / `setOnChangedScript` / `setOnRemovedScript` / `setValidateFunction`.
**Properties:**
```js def.getProperties() def.getProperty("Verb") def.hasProperty("Verb") def.addProperty("Verb") // create + return IStereotypePropertyDefinitionApi def.removeProperty("Verb") // throws if absent def.delete() // remove the whole definition ```
#### 3. A property (`IStereotypePropertyDefinitionApi`)
`addProperty` returns it; each readonly field has a `setX()`.
```js p.setName(v) p.setComment(v) p.setHint(v) p.setDefaultValue("GET") p.setPlaceholder("e.g. GET") p.setIsActiveFunction(js) p.setIsRequiredFunction(js) // JS controlling visibility / required-ness ```
**Control type** — the editor widget. `setControlType` one of: `"text-box"` | `"text-area"` | `"checkbox"` | `"number"` | `"decimal"` | `"select"` | `"multi-select"` | `"icon"` | `"item-list"` | `"javascript-function"`.
```js p.setControlType("text-box") ```
For `"select"` / `"multi-select"` you ALSO declare where options come from — `setOptionsSource` one of:
- `"options"` (static list) → `p.setValueOptions(["GET","POST","PUT"])` - `"lookup-element"` / `"nested-lookup"` → `p.setLookupTypes(["Class"])` (element-settings type names/ids) - `"lookup-children"` → `p.setLookupChildrenRootFunction(js returning a root element id)` - `"lookup-stereotype"` | `"not-applicable"`
```js p.setOptionsSource("options"); p.setValueOptions(["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"]); ```
- `"item-list"` → `p.setItemType("<stereotype-def name or id>")` (each item is an instance of that definition) - `"javascript-function"` → `p.setApiTypeDefinition("element-macro")` (which API surface the JS gets: `"element-direct"` | `"element-macro"` | `"element-visual"` | `"association-direct"` | `"association-macro"` | `"mapping-element"` | `"mapping-macro"` | `"module-settings"` | `"stereotype-display"`)
```js p.delete() ```
#### 4. Worked recipe
Define an "Http Settings" stereotype on Commands, with a Verb dropdown and a Route text box:
```js const pkg = lookupPackage("MyModule"); const def = pkg.getStereotypeDefinition("Http Settings") ?? pkg.addStereotypeDefinition("Http Settings"); def.setComment("HTTP routing for a command"); def.setIcon("fa-globe"); def.setTargetMode("elements-of-type"); def.setTargetTypes(["Command"]); def.setApplyMode("manually"); const verb = def.getProperty("Verb") ?? def.addProperty("Verb"); verb.setControlType("select"); verb.setOptionsSource("options"); verb.setValueOptions(["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"]); verb.setDefaultValue("POST"); const route = def.getProperty("Route") ?? def.addProperty("Route"); route.setControlType("text-box"); route.setPlaceholder("e.g. /orders/{id}"); console.log(`Defined ${def.name} with ${def.getProperties().length} properties`); ```
After running: inspect the returned `errors[]` (scoped to what you changed) and fix any in a follow-up script.
